currentState: Rapid professional respect born under pressure. Muradin reads Sylvanas as the field commander of the pair that reached him, and Sylvanas reads Muradin as the last local intelligence source worth trusting all the way to the cave.
powerDynamic: Sylvanas controls the incoming formation and the eventual retreat call. Muradin controls the local map, the warning, and the route to Frostmourne. Neither wastes time contesting the other's lane.

physicalDynamics.pattern: Their first dynamic is all measure and acceptance at the camp gate: Muradin reading rank and intent in a glance, Sylvanas giving him enough stillness to do it.
By the retreat the relationship has become literal weight-sharing, with Sylvanas helping haul Muradin out when his own legs are no longer part of the equation.

nameUsage.sylvanasCallsMuradin: Uses Muradin plainly once his value is established; there is no ornamental formality between them.
nameUsage.muradinCallsSylvanas: Treats her as commander rather than court title, recognizing competence first.

history.event: Muradin lets Sylvanas's party into camp because her bearing and urgency read as legitimate command, not opportunism.
Sylvanas accepts Muradin's route and curse warning at once, treating him as a hard-source witness rather than a provincial obstacle.
The cave loss converts that professional respect into rescue duty when Sylvanas prioritizes extracting Muradin alive over any fantasy of salvaging the fight.

trajectory: Strangers -> immediate field respect -> chapter 21's rescue bond formed under a shared failed attempt to stop Arthas.
